content: Tallow is a stable fat that doesn’t oxidize under heat, unlike unstable omega-6 fats in seed oils.
Tallow contains stearic acid, which supports mitochondrial function and fat oxidation.
Tallow includes conjugated linoleic acid (CLA), which provides metabolic support.
Tallow contains vitamins A, D, E, and K in fat-soluble forms that the body can use.
Tallow has a high smoke point of ~400°F, making it suitable for various cooking methods.
Grass-fed, grass-finished, regeneratively-raised beef tallow is recommended over industrial sources.
